mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals stronger manufacturing activity, which typically precedes improved corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of rising INDPRO are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% indicates tight labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of UNRATE < 5% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the signal's predictive power vanishes during high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and CPI (CPIAUCSL) indicates strong demand pressures with inflationary momentum, which historically correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days due to risk-on sentiment.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of concurrent GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL growth are negative in > 40% of cases or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A 10-basis-point increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) during a tightening cycle compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of FEDFUNDS increases exceed 1% or the signal's Rank IC drops below -0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 100k signals robust job market expansion that supports consumer confidence and corporate hiring, driving S&P 500 returns positively over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of PAYEMS > 100k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's predictive power breaks during recessionary signals.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 650k reflects strong residential demand that boosts construction sector activity and consumer wealth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of HOUST > 650k are negative in > 30% of cases or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a favorable environment for equity valuations, producing S&P 500 returns with a positive direction over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of RSAFS growth and FEDFUNDS < 1% are negative in > 50% of cases or the interaction term's coefficient loses significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average indicates weakening supply-side dynamics that erode corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of INDPRO below 12mo MA are positive in > 40% of cases or the signal's predictive power disappears during stagflationary conditions.
